title: Scope and Purpose
text:
Document Title: Scope and Purpose
Section: Overview

The Playage Backoffice System is the centralized administrative suite for orchestrating the entire gaming platform. It gives authorized operators a single control surface to configure system settings, monitor live performance, track finances, manage partners, and ensure compliance. Every operational area—user activity, payments, gaming products, CRM, and infrastructure health—feeds into this console so that teams can make quick, well-informed decisions.

title: Scope and Purpose
text:
Document Title: Scope and Purpose
Section: Purpose

The Backoffice exists to keep day-to-day operations efficient, transparent, and auditable. Core goals include:
Real-time performance monitoring to guarantee that services stay stable and responsive.
User and partner administration with configurable roles, permissions, and access history.
Financial oversight covering deposits, withdrawals, balance reconciliation, and payouts.
Product management for sportsbook, casino, live games, and third-party integrations.
CRM & promotions to manage campaigns, loyalty programs, promo codes, and notifications.
Analytics & reporting that provide on-demand dashboards for business stakeholders and regulators.

title: Scope and Purpose
text:

Section: Scope

This documentation set focuses on the main administrative capabilities inside the Playage Backoffice:
Platform monitoring: Track infrastructure health, scheduled tasks, and security alerts in real time.
User & role management: Create, update, or deactivate operator accounts, apply RBAC, and audit session history.
Player operations: View verified documents, enforce responsible-gaming rules, and investigate transactions.
Financial tracking: Reconcile payments, follow revenue KPIs, and export financial data for accounting teams.
Sportsbook & casino operations: Configure markets, odds, game catalogs, and provider-level settings.
CRM & promotions: Launch marketing campaigns, automate bonus eligibility, and deliver targeted communications.
System configuration & maintenance: Tune platform parameters, manage email templates, and schedule upgrades/backups.

Document Title: Getting Started
Section: Getting Started

The Getting Started section provides essential guidance for new administrators, operators, and partners using the Playage Backoffice — the unified control system for managing all platform operations. This section is designed to introduce the core functionalities, help you set up your account, and guide you through the key interface elements to ensure efficient use from the first session.
By following these instructions, users will gain a clear understanding of the platform's structure, interface, and security best practices.

title: Getting Started
text:
Document Title: Getting Started
Section: 2.1.1 Login and Authentication

Access to the Playage Backoffice is restricted to authorized users. For secure and smooth login, each administrator must follow these steps:
Open the Official Backoffice URL in your browser.
Enter Your Registered Email Address and Password .
Click "Sign In" to begin the authentication process.
Two-Factor Authentication (2FA): If enabled, enter the verification code sent to your registered email or generated by your authenticator app (e.g., Google Authenticator).
Once authentication is successful, you will be redirected to the Main Dashboard .
Use only company-approved browsers (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) to access the Backoffice.
Never share your login credentials or verification codes with others.
If you encounter multiple failed login attempts , contact your system administrator to reset your password or verify your account role.
Automatic Session Timeout: Sessions will expire after a period of inactivity to protect your account.
Tip: For added protection, enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) from the Admin Settings → Security Panel .

To ensure the security of your account and prevent unauthorized access, always log out after completing your administrative tasks.
Click your profile icon in the top-right corner of the dashboard.

Below are the major field groups included in the Player Data Table:
Player ID – Unique system-generated identifier for each player.
Username – The login name registered by the player.
First Name / Last Name / Middle Name – Player's full name components.
Personal ID – National or system-issued identity reference.
Email – Primary registered email address.
Mobile / Phone – Contact information associated with the account.
Register Date – Date when the account was created.
Verified – Indicates whether the player's KYC verification is complete.
Document Number / Type / Issue Date / Expiration Date / Issued By – Official document verification details.
2FA Verification – Shows whether two-factor authentication is enabled.
QR Code Is Applied – Status of QR-based account identification.
Address / City / State / Zip Code / Country – Player's residence details.
Birth City / Birth Department / Birth Date – Personal birth record details.
Citizenship – Player's country of citizenship.
Gender – Registered gender information.
Title – Optional title prefix (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.).
Affiliate ID – Reference to the affiliate partner or campaign source.
BTag / Promo Code – Used for tracking marketing and promotional attribution.
Registration Source – Origin channel (e.g., web, mobile, API).
Last Login Date / Last Wrong Login Date / Wrong Login Attempts – User access logs for monitoring login behavior.
External ID – Unique external mapping identifier (used for integrations).
RF-ID – Linked RFID reference (if enabled in system integration).
Balance – Displays the player's current wallet balance.
Is Email Subscribed – Indicates whether the user opted for email updates.
Subscribed to Newsletter / Push Notifications – Displays marketing communication preferences.
TC Version Acceptance Date – Latest Terms & Conditions acceptance record.
Custom Player Category – Segment or label applied manually or via automation.
Player Category – Default system-assigned classification.
AML Status – Anti-Money Laundering check result.
PEP Status – Politically Exposed Person check status.
Income Source – Player's declared source of income.
Exclusion Type / Self Excluded – Details of any responsible gaming or exclusion settings.
Is Casino Blocked / Is Sport Blocked / Is RMT Blocked – Restrictions applied to specific gaming categories.
Verify By – Admin username who verified the player's identity.
Action – Set of icons to view, edit, or manage a player's record directly.

The Self-Exclusion Portal is a regulatory-compliance tool that provides full visibility into players under self-restriction . It assists the Responsible Gaming Team in enforcing exclusion policies, tracking reactivation timelines, and generating compliance reports.

Enforce responsible-gaming protocols and self-exclusion regulations.

Streamline communication between compliance and support teams.

Automate alerts for upcoming expiration or policy violations.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:
Document Title: Role and Permission Management
Section: 1. Introduction

The User Role Permission Management Console is a powerful feature that allows administrators to define, edit, and manage user roles and their associated permissions within the Playage Admin Console . This tool ensures that users are granted access only to the resources they need, maintaining security and control within the platform. Roles can be customized, and permissions can be dynamically applied to users based on their roles, ensuring an organized and secure access control system.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:
Document Title: Role and Permission Management
Section: 2. Purpose

The purpose of the User Role Permission Management Console is to:
Provide a centralized view of all roles configured in the system.
Allow administrators to assign, remove, and manage permissions for specific roles.
Maintain security by ensuring that each user has the appropriate level of access based on their role.
Enable auditing and control by tracking changes to roles and permissions.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:
Document Title: Role and Permission Management
Section: 3. Interface Overview

The console is divided into two main sections:
Permissions Panel (on the left): Displays the available permissions for each module in the system, categorized into sections like Dashboard, Players, Reports, etc.
Users Panel (on the right): Displays the roles assigned to the users and allows for easy assignment of permissions.
Key Elements:
Not Given : This section lists the available permissions that are not yet assigned to the selected role.
Given : This section shows the permissions already assigned to the selected role.
Search Bar : Allows for filtering and searching for roles and permissions dynamically.
Action Buttons : Buttons for adding, removing, and editing roles and permissions.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:
Document Title: Role and Permission Management
Section: 4. Key Features

Role Management : Admins can create new roles, edit existing roles, and assign them to users.
Permission Assignment : Admins can assign or remove permissions for each role, allowing them to control access to various parts of the system.
Dynamic Role Updates : Changes made to roles and permissions are applied dynamically and immediately reflect on the assigned users.
Audit Tracking : Every change to roles and permissions is logged and timestamped for audit purposes.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:
Document Title: Role and Permission Management
Section: 5. On-Screen Elements

Role Name Column : Displays the name of each role (e.g., Admin, Risk Manager, Partner).
Users Column : Shows the number of users assigned to each role.
Last Update Column : Displays the most recent date and time of modification for each role.
Action Column : Provides buttons for editing (🖉) or deleting (🗑) roles.
Search Bar : Located at the top-right of the page, this allows admins to search for roles or permissions by name.
Add Role Button : Located at the top-right, this button opens a panel to create a new role and assign permissions.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:
Document Title: Role and Permission Management
Section: 6. How to Use

Step 1: Access the Role Permission Management Console
Navigate to the User Management → Role Permission Console in the admin interface.
Step 2: Create a New Role
Click on the "Add Role" button (red button) located at the top-right of the page.
Enter a Role Name (e.g., "Marketing Manager").
Select the permissions for the role by checking the relevant boxes in the Permissions Panel .
Once you've selected the permissions, click Save . The new role will appear in the role list with "0 users" until assigned to users.
Step 3: Edit an Existing Role
Locate the role you want to modify from the role list.
Click on the Edit (🖉) icon next to the role.
Adjust the permissions as needed by selecting or unselecting the appropriate boxes.
Once done, click Save to apply the changes, which will reflect under the "Last Update" column.
Step 4: Delete a Role
Find the role you wish to delete.
Click on the Delete (🗑) icon next to the role.
Confirm the deletion. (Roles that are unassigned can be deleted.)
Step 5: Assign Users to a Role
Go to the User List page.
Edit a user's account and select the target role from the dropdown menu.
The user count will automatically update in the Role Permission Console.
Step 6: Managing Permissions for a Role
Once a role is selected in the Role Name Column , its associated permissions can be viewed and edited directly within the Permissions Panel .
Admins can assign or remove permissions by clicking on the corresponding options.

Permissions Tracking : All changes to roles and permissions are automatically timestamped for future reference.
Super Admin Access : Only Super Admins can modify or delete other roles.

title: Role and Permission Management
text:

Enforce Role-Based Segregation : Ensure that roles are clearly defined with appropriate permissions, preventing privilege overlap.
Minimal Access Principle : Grant only the permissions that are strictly necessary for a role's functionality.
Regular Review : Periodically review and update roles and permissions to maintain the integrity of the system.
Audit Custom Roles : Ensure that every custom role is documented for traceability during audits.

The User Role Permission Management Console is a crucial tool for maintaining secure and organized user access within the Playage Admin Console . By allowing admins to manage roles, permissions, and user assignments dynamically, it ensures that only authorized users have access to specific areas of the platform, improving security and control.

title: Two-Factor Authentication
text:
Document Title: Two-Factor Authentication
Section: How to Enable Google Authenticator (TOTP)

Click Enable Now next to Google Authenticator.
A QR code and backup key appear on the screen.
Open Google Authenticator or a similar TOTP app.
Scan the QR code or manually enter the backup key.
Enter the 6-digit verification code from your app.
On success, 2FA status changes to Enabled .
Save the backup key securely — this is required if you lose access to your device.
Notes:
If the QR code doesn't appear, refresh and ensure pop-ups are allowed.
TOTP codes are time-based — make sure your phone's clock is synchronized.

title: Two-Factor Authentication
text:
Document Title: Two-Factor Authentication
Section: How to Enable Email Authenticator

Click Enable Now beside Email Authenticator.
A verification code is sent to your registered email.
Enter the code and click Verify / Confirm .
Email 2FA is then activated for future logins.
Notes:
Verify your email is current and can receive messages from @playagegaming.tech .
Email codes may take up to 1–2 minutes to arrive.

title: Two-Factor Authentication
text:
Document Title: Two-Factor Authentication
Section: Disable / Remove 2FA

When 2FA is active, a Disable / Remove option appears.
You must authenticate using your 2FA code before disabling.
Always log and authorize any 2FA deactivation for security compliance.

Best Practice: Create a secondary admin account with 2FA for emergency recovery.
